stories.headline: US to blockade Iranian ports, risking wider war over Strait of Hormuz
stories.summary: US‑Iran talks in Pakistan have collapsed as Trump announced the blockade of Iranian ports and the Strait of Hormuz starting on Monday afternoon, stoking war fears and pushing oil prices sharply higher.

stories.headline: Iran Threatens Ports as Trump Vows Hormuz Blockade
stories.summary: Iran warned it would target all ports in and close to the Persian Gulf if its own shipping hubs are threatened, after US President Donald Trump announced plans for a blockade of Tehran-linked vessels in the Strait of Hormuz. Tyler Kendall reports on Bloomberg Television. (Source: Bloomberg)

stories.headline: Iran War: Trump Vows to Blockade Hormuz & Hungary's Orban Concedes Defeat | The Pulse 4/13
stories.summary:
"The Pulse With Francine Lacqua" is all about conversations with high profile guests in the beating heart of global business, economics, finance and politics. Based in London, we go wherever the story is, bringing you exclusive interviews and market-moving scoops.

Today's guests: Dan Scott, Chief Investment Officer and Head of Multi Asset at Vontobel Asset Management, Sherrill Stroschein, Politics Professor at University College London & Elisabe

stories.headline: Pope Leo, Hours After Trump Criticism, Says He Has ‘No Fear’
stories.summary: President Trump lashed out on Sunday night at Pope Leo XIV, who responded hours later by saying he would continue to oppose war.

stories.headline: Pope responds to president’s insults: ‘I have no fear of the Trump administration’
stories.summary: The president wrote that the first U.S.-born pope, who has criticized the Iran war, was “weak” and “terrible” on crime and foreign policy.

stories.headline: Pope Leo says he does not fear Trump after attack over Iran peace appeal
stories.summary: Leo, ​who last year became the first US-born pope, has emerged as an outspoken critic of the US-Israeli ⁠war on Iran.

stories.headline: Trump lambasts Pope Leo XIV in ongoging feud with Catholic Church leader over Iran war
stories.summary: The US president launches an unprecedented attack on Pope Leo XIV over the pontiff's rejection of the Iran war, accusing him of being "weak on crime" and hurting the Catholic Church.

stories.headline: Pope says he has 'no fear' of Trump after scathing criticism
stories.summary: The US president accuses Leo of being "weak on crime and terrible for foreign policy".

stories.headline: Two Traders Convicted in Paris Over $23 Million Insider Coup
stories.summary: A Paris court convicted two men of insider trading in a secret network that made $23 million on a tip from a former Societe Generale SA banker about a confidential multibillion-dollar acquisition.

stories.headline: Hungary Foreign Minister Shredding EU Documents, Magyar Says
stories.summary: Peter Magyar accused outgoing Hungarian Foreign Minister Peter Szijjarto of destroying documents related to European Union sanctions.

stories.headline: Goldman Shares Fall as Bond Trading Miss Outweighs Equity Record
stories.summary: Goldman Sachs Group Inc.’s equity traders beat their own previous all-time quarterly high by more than $1 billion, as the war in Iran fueled market volatility, offsetting a disappointing haul by their fixed-income colleagues.

stories.headline: Morgan Stanley Says Earnings Shield S&P 500 From Iran War
stories.summary: Accelerating earnings are protecting the S&P 500 from deeper losses and masking a broader pullback in US equities, according to strategists at Morgan Stanley.

stories.headline: Stock Market Trading Shows Fear of Risk Despite Weeks of Gains
stories.summary: On the surface, it appears that the stock market has been on a tear for the past couple of weeks. Look under the hood, however, and you’ll see that investors are buying but without the enthusiasm that usually marks the start of a major rally.

stories.headline: What We Know About the Eric Swalwell Sexual Misconduct Allegations

stories.headline: India’s Shapoorji Seeks to Delay Paying $1.5 Billion of Debt
stories.summary: Shapoorji Pallonji Group, the Indian conglomerate behind the nation’s biggest-ever private credit deal, is trying to delay paying a separate debt, people familiar with the matter said, underscoring mounting strains amid volatile markets.

stories.headline: China Credit Slowdown Worse Than Forecast as Loan Demand Weakens
stories.summary: China’s credit expansion slowed more than expected from a year earlier, as continued weakness in household and business demand holds back borrowing.

stories.headline: Protesters rally in Paris against ‘liberty-killing’ anti-Semitism bill

Massive Attack’s Robert Del Naja arrested at Palestine Action protest in London
stories.summary: Massive Attack frontman Robert Del Naja was among 500 people arrested at Palestine Action protest in London this weekend. The celebrated trip-hop musician said he wanted to attend the protest despite the consequences a potential arrest could have on his career and touring.

stories.headline: Thinking about moving to Ireland? This entire village, complete with a spa, is up for sale
stories.summary: The Village at Lyons, once owned by Ryanair founder Tony Ryan, has 47 bedrooms and is on the market for a cool €20 million.

stories.headline: Poland and South Korea upgrade ties to strategic defence partnership deal
stories.summary: During a visit to Seoul on Monday, Polish Prime Minister Donald Tusk and South Korean President Lee Jae-myung pledged to strengthen their cooperation, particularly in defence.

stories.headline: Trump announces naval blockade of Iran after Islamabad talks yield no deal
stories.summary: The blockade could derail a tenuous ceasefire after just five days. Vice President JD Vance said the U.S. remains open to diplomacy if Iran takes “our final and best offer.”

stories.headline: Ukraine and Russia accuse each other of hundreds of ceasefire violations
stories.summary: Ukrainian President Volodymyr Zelensky says his nation's forces would respond "symmetrically" to Russian attacks.

stories.headline: Hungary’s Viktor Orban, ally of Trump and Putin, concedes election defeat
stories.summary: WIth record turnout, Hungarians chose to end the 16-year rule of the prime minister who was a self-proclaimed champion of illiberal Christian democracy.

stories.headline: Political turmoil in Indian border state as nine million lose voting rights

stories.headline: Stocks Drop as Oil Tops $100 and Goldman Slides: Markets Wrap
stories.summary: Oil jumped above $100 a barrel while stocks and bonds fell after President Donald Trump ordered a blockade of the Strait of Hormuz following the weekend’s deadlock in US-Iran peace talks. Goldman Sachs Group Inc. shares dropped in an underwhelming start to the earnings season.
